body:
Our strain and stress formulation is not metric-aware: i.e. it's correct on a rectilinear grid, but it fails to conserve energy on a curvillinear grid. This PR introduces the SI3 formulation for strain and stress computed as a function of derived fields such as divergence $(\delta)$ tension $(\mathcal{T})$ and shear $(\mathcal{S})$.
In particular the strain rate is computed as

$$\dot{\epsilon}_{xx} = \delta + \mathcal{T}$$
$$\dot{\epsilon}_{yy} = \delta - \mathcal{T}$$
$$\dot{\epsilon}_{xy} = \mathcal{S} / 2$$

while the ice stress divergence is a function of the divergent stress invariant $\sigma\delta = \sigma_{11} + \sigma_{22}$, the tension component $\sigma\mathcal{T} = \sigma_{11} - \sigma_{22}$ and the tangential stress $\sigma_{12}$.
This formulation satisfies the discrete energy budget of the momentum equation on curvilinear grids and is guaranteed to not inject numerical noise at the grid scale
title: Metric-aware formulation for strain and stress
